name: directory
explanation: The Directory check is a generic filesystem-scanning template: the user MUST supply an absolute path in `directory`, and the check walks it with `os.walk`, calling `stat()` on matching files to emit gauges/histograms (sizes, mtimes, file/folder counts). There is no upstream service, no port, no protocol, and no canonical default path — every deployment monitors a different user-chosen directory (logs dir, upload dir, queue dir, etc.). The `directory` field is the entire purpose of the integration; auto-config has no signal from which to synthesize it. This is pattern J ("user-supplied generic check template"): the integration is itself the configuration template.
notes: Pattern J. The check requires user *intent* (which directory matters) — that input is policy, not discoverable state. Method `other` because no probe exists; method is not `credentials-required` since no auth is involved.
